speaker: LEONTES

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Since what I am to say must be but that

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Which contradicts my accusation, and

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The testimony on my part no other

speaker: HERMIONE
text: But what comes from myself, it shall scarce boot me

speaker: HERMIONE
text: To say “Not guilty.” Mine integrity,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Being counted falsehood, shall, as I express it,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Be so received. But thus: if powers divine

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Behold our human actions, as they do,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I doubt not then but innocence shall make

speaker: HERMIONE
text: False accusation blush and tyranny

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Tremble at patience. You, my lord, best know,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Whom least will seem to do so, my past life

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Hath been as continent, as chaste, as true,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: As I am now unhappy; which is more

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Than history can pattern, though devised

speaker: HERMIONE
text: And played to take spectators. For behold me,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: A fellow of the royal bed, which owe

speaker: HERMIONE
text: A moiety of the throne, a great king’s daughter,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The mother to a hopeful prince, here standing

speaker: HERMIONE
text: To prate and talk for life and honor fore

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Who please to come and hear. For life, I prize it

speaker: HERMIONE
text: As I weigh grief, which I would spare. For honor,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: ’Tis a derivative from me to mine,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: And only that I stand for. I appeal

speaker: HERMIONE
text: To your own conscience, sir, before Polixenes

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Came to your court, how I was in your grace,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: How merited to be so; since he came,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: With what encounter so uncurrent I

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Have strained t’ appear thus; if one jot beyond

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The bound of honor, or in act or will

speaker: HERMIONE
text: That way inclining, hardened be the hearts

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Of all that hear me, and my near’st of kin

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Cry fie upon my grave.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I ne’er heard yet

speaker: LEONTES
text: That any of these bolder vices wanted

speaker: LEONTES
text: Less impudence to gainsay what they did

speaker: LEONTES
text: Than to perform it first.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: That’s true enough,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Though ’tis a saying, sir, not due to me.

speaker: LEONTES
text: You will not own it.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: More than mistress of

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Which comes to me in name of fault, I must not

speaker: HERMIONE
text: At all acknowledge. For Polixenes,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: With whom I am accused, I do confess

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I loved him as in honor he required,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: With such a kind of love as might become

speaker: HERMIONE
text: A lady like me, with a love even such,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: So and no other, as yourself commanded,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Which not to have done, I think, had been in me

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Both disobedience and ingratitude

speaker: HERMIONE
text: To you and toward your friend, whose love had spoke,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Even since it could speak, from an infant, freely

speaker: HERMIONE
text: That it was yours. Now, for conspiracy,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I know not how it tastes, though it be dished

speaker: HERMIONE
text: For me to try how. All I know of it

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Is that Camillo was an honest man;

speaker: HERMIONE
text: And why he left your court, the gods themselves,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Wotting no more than I, are ignorant.

speaker: LEONTES
text: You knew of his departure, as you know

speaker: LEONTES
text: What you have underta’en to do in ’s absence.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Sir,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: You speak a language that I understand not.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: My life stands in the level of your dreams,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Which I’ll lay down.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Your actions are my dreams.

speaker: LEONTES
text: You had a bastard by Polixenes,

speaker: LEONTES
text: And I but dreamed it. As you were past all shame—

speaker: LEONTES
text: Those of your fact are so—so past all truth,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Which to deny concerns more than avails; for as

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thy brat hath been cast out, like to itself,

speaker: LEONTES
text: No father owning it—which is indeed

speaker: LEONTES
text: More criminal in thee than it—so thou

speaker: LEONTES
text: Shalt feel our justice, in whose easiest passage

speaker: LEONTES
text: Look for no less than death.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Sir, spare your threats.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The bug which you would fright me with I seek.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: To me can life be no commodity.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The crown and comfort of my life, your favor,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I do give lost, for I do feel it gone,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: But know not how it went. My second joy

speaker: HERMIONE
text: And first fruits of my body, from his presence

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I am barred like one infectious. My third comfort,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Starred most unluckily, is from my breast,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The innocent milk in it most innocent mouth,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Haled out to murder; myself on every post

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Proclaimed a strumpet; with immodest hatred

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The childbed privilege denied, which longs

speaker: HERMIONE
text: To women of all fashion; lastly, hurried

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Here to this place, i’ th’ open air, before

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I have got strength of limit. Now, my liege,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Tell me what blessings I have here alive,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: That I should fear to die? Therefore proceed.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: But yet hear this (mistake me not: no life,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I prize it not a straw, but for mine honor,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Which I would free), if I shall be condemned

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Upon surmises, all proofs sleeping else

speaker: HERMIONE
text: But what your jealousies awake, I tell you

speaker: HERMIONE
text: ’Tis rigor, and not law. Your Honors all,

speaker: HERMIONE
text: I do refer me to the oracle.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Apollo be my judge.

speaker: LORD
text: This your request

speaker: LORD
text: Is altogether just. Therefore bring forth,

speaker: LORD
text: And in Apollo’s name, his oracle.

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The Emperor of Russia was my father.

speaker: HERMIONE

speaker: HERMIONE
text: His daughter’s trial, that he did but see

speaker: HERMIONE
text: The flatness of my misery, yet with eyes

speaker: HERMIONE
text: Of pity, not revenge.

speaker: LORDS
text: Now blessèd be the great Apollo!

speaker: HERMIONE

speaker: LEONTES
text: Hast thou read truth?

speaker: OFFICER
text: Ay, my lord, even so as it is here set down.

speaker: LEONTES
text: There is no truth at all i’ th’ oracle.

speaker: LEONTES
text: The sessions shall proceed. This is mere falsehood.

speaker: SERVANT
text: My lord the King, the King!

speaker: LEONTES
text: What is the business?

speaker: SERVANT
text: O sir, I shall be hated to report it.

speaker: SERVANT
text: The Prince your son, with mere conceit and fear

speaker: SERVANT
text: Of the Queen’s speed, is gone.

speaker: LEONTES
text: How? Gone?

speaker: SERVANT
text: Is dead.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Apollo’s angry, and the heavens themselves

speaker: LEONTES
text: Do strike at my injustice.

speaker: LEONTES
text: How now there?

speaker: PAULINA
text: This news is mortal to the Queen. Look down

speaker: PAULINA
text: And see what death is doing.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Take her hence.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Her heart is but o’ercharged. She will recover.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I have too much believed mine own suspicion.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Beseech you, tenderly apply to her

speaker: LEONTES
text: Some remedies for life.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Apollo, pardon

speaker: LEONTES
text: My great profaneness ’gainst thine oracle.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I’ll reconcile me to Polixenes,

speaker: LEONTES
text: New woo my queen, recall the good Camillo,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Whom I proclaim a man of truth, of mercy;

speaker: LEONTES
text: For, being transported by my jealousies

speaker: LEONTES
text: To bloody thoughts and to revenge, I chose

speaker: LEONTES
text: Camillo for the minister to poison

speaker: LEONTES
text: My friend Polixenes, which had been done

speaker: LEONTES
text: But that the good mind of Camillo tardied

speaker: LEONTES
text: My swift command, though I with death and with

speaker: LEONTES
text: Reward did threaten and encourage him,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Not doing it and being done. He, most humane

speaker: LEONTES
text: And filled with honor, to my kingly guest

speaker: LEONTES
text: Unclasped my practice, quit his fortunes here,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Which you knew great, and to the hazard

speaker: LEONTES
text: Of all incertainties himself commended,

speaker: LEONTES
text: No richer than his honor. How he glisters

speaker: LEONTES
text: Through my rust, and how his piety

speaker: LEONTES
text: Does my deeds make the blacker!

speaker: PAULINA
text: Woe the while!

speaker: PAULINA
text: O, cut my lace, lest my heart, cracking it,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Break too!

speaker: LORD
text: What fit is this, good lady?

speaker: PAULINA
text: What studied torments, tyrant, hast for me?

speaker: PAULINA
text: What wheels, racks, fires? What flaying? Boiling

speaker: PAULINA
text: In leads or oils? What old or newer torture

speaker: PAULINA
text: Must I receive, whose every word deserves

speaker: PAULINA
text: To taste of thy most worst? Thy tyranny,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Together working with thy jealousies,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Fancies too weak for boys, too green and idle

speaker: PAULINA
text: For girls of nine, O, think what they have done,

speaker: PAULINA
text: And then run mad indeed, stark mad, for all

speaker: PAULINA
text: Thy bygone fooleries were but spices of it.

speaker: PAULINA
text: That thou betrayedst Polixenes, ’twas nothing;

speaker: PAULINA
text: That did but show thee of a fool, inconstant

speaker: PAULINA
text: And damnable ingrateful. Nor was ’t much

speaker: PAULINA
text: Thou wouldst have poisoned good Camillo’s honor,

speaker: PAULINA
text: To have him kill a king: poor trespasses,

speaker: PAULINA
text: More monstrous standing by, whereof I reckon

speaker: PAULINA
text: The casting forth to crows thy baby daughter

speaker: PAULINA
text: To be or none or little, though a devil

speaker: PAULINA
text: Would have shed water out of fire ere done ’t.

speaker: PAULINA
text: Nor is ’t directly laid to thee the death

speaker: PAULINA
text: Of the young prince, whose honorable thoughts,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Thoughts high for one so tender, cleft the heart

speaker: PAULINA
text: That could conceive a gross and foolish sire

speaker: PAULINA
text: Blemished his gracious dam. This is not, no,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Laid to thy answer. But the last—O lords,

speaker: PAULINA
text: When I have said, cry woe!—the Queen, the Queen,

speaker: PAULINA
text: The sweet’st, dear’st creature’s dead, and vengeance for ’t

speaker: PAULINA
text: Not dropped down yet.

speaker: LORD
text: The higher powers forbid!

speaker: PAULINA
text: I say she’s dead. I’ll swear ’t. If word nor oath

speaker: PAULINA
text: Prevail not, go and see. If you can bring

speaker: PAULINA
text: Tincture or luster in her lip, her eye,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Heat outwardly or breath within, I’ll serve you

speaker: PAULINA
text: As I would do the gods.—But, O thou tyrant,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Do not repent these things, for they are heavier

speaker: PAULINA
text: Than all thy woes can stir. Therefore betake thee

speaker: PAULINA
text: To nothing but despair. A thousand knees

speaker: PAULINA
text: Ten thousand years together, naked, fasting,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Upon a barren mountain, and still winter

speaker: PAULINA
text: In storm perpetual, could not move the gods

speaker: PAULINA
text: To look that way thou wert.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Go on, go on.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou canst not speak too much. I have deserved

speaker: LEONTES
text: All tongues to talk their bitt’rest.

speaker: LORD
text: Say no more.

speaker: LORD
text: Howe’er the business goes, you have made fault

speaker: LORD
text: I’ th’ boldness of your speech.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I am sorry for ’t.

speaker: PAULINA
text: All faults I make, when I shall come to know them,

speaker: PAULINA
text: I do repent. Alas, I have showed too much

speaker: PAULINA
text: The rashness of a woman. He is touched

speaker: PAULINA
text: To th’ noble heart.—What’s gone and what’s past help

speaker: PAULINA
text: Should be past grief. Do not receive affliction

speaker: PAULINA
text: At my petition. I beseech you, rather

speaker: PAULINA
text: Let me be punished, that have minded you

speaker: PAULINA
text: Of what you should forget. Now, good my liege,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Sir, royal sir, forgive a foolish woman.

speaker: PAULINA
text: The love I bore your queen—lo, fool again!—

speaker: PAULINA
text: I’ll speak of her no more, nor of your children.

speaker: PAULINA
text: I’ll not remember you of my own lord,

speaker: PAULINA
text: Who is lost too. Take your patience to you,

speaker: PAULINA
text: And I’ll say nothing.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou didst speak but well

speaker: LEONTES
text: When most the truth, which I receive much better

speaker: LEONTES
text: Than to be pitied of thee. Prithee, bring me

speaker: LEONTES
text: To the dead bodies of my queen and son.

speaker: LEONTES
text: One grave shall be for both. Upon them shall

speaker: LEONTES
text: The causes of their death appear, unto

speaker: LEONTES
text: Our shame perpetual. Once a day I’ll visit

speaker: LEONTES
text: The chapel where they lie, and tears shed there

speaker: LEONTES
text: Shall be my recreation. So long as nature

speaker: LEONTES
text: Will bear up with this exercise, so long

speaker: LEONTES
text: I daily vow to use it. Come, and lead me

speaker: LEONTES
text: To these sorrows.
